mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.5 million signals an increase in supply of new homes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in the CPI above 3% signals a monetary easing cycle with rising inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds and rising CPI are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in initial jobless claims above 250k signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising initial jobless claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real GDP signals a weakening aggregate demand for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining real GDP are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real sales above 6.8 million signals an increase in supply of new homes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real sales are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
